title: The Clint Eastwood Mt. Rushmore, and the Curious Case of  ‘Juror No. 2.’ Plus: ‘Heretic’!
format: Sean Fennessy and Adam Nayman on Warner Bros. burying Clint Eastwood's 'Juror No. 2' in 38 theaters, then a Mount Rushmore of Clint roles. After that Sean reviews 'Heretic' and talks with its writer-directors, Scott Beck and Brian Woods.

segments.heading: The Curious Case of ‘Juror No. 2’
segments.note: Sean Fennessy & Adam Nayman on Clint's near-buried courtroom drama

films.title: Juror No. 2
films.note: A recovering-alcoholic juror realizes he may have caused the death the defendant is on trial for. Nicholas Hoult, Toni Collette, J.K. Simmons, Kiefer Sutherland; a 12 Angry Men riff that ends on Collette at the door and cuts before she speaks. Warner Bros. put it in fewer than 50 theaters with no wide plan, the crime Nayman finds Warner guilty of.

films.title: The Mule
films.note: Cited against the studio's logic: a bona fide original hit Eastwood drew audiences to in his late 80s.

films.title: Richard Jewell
films.note: The late-Clint Nayman likes, and the closest cousin to Juror No. 2: another man wrongly caught in the machinery of an institution.

films.title: Sully
films.note: Same template: a man put on trial by the institution he served, seeking restitution.

films.title: American Sniper
films.note: Nayman's least favorite of the run; he'll grant the second unit the battle scenes but not the moral center.

films.title: The 15:17 to Paris
films.note: The weird spiritual doppelganger of American Sniper, and to Nayman the better version of the story.

films.title: Mystic River
films.note: Nayman's dissent on the anointed early-2000s Clint: he thinks it sucks and hasn't held up.

films.title: Cry Macho
films.note: The last time Sean, Nayman, and Chris Ryan gathered to talk Clint, and, at $30 million, wrongly written off as a waste.

films.title: Absolute Power
films.note: Invoked for Judy Davis's cover-up-everything DA, the caricature the Toni Collette prosecutor pointedly is not.

films.title: Conclave
films.note: The rival in the room: a papal mystery already past $15 million, chasing the same adult audience Juror No. 2 was denied.

films.title: 12 Angry Men
films.note: The template: one juror who puts himself in the defendant's box. Still one of Letterboxd's highest-rated films; Hoult reaches Fonda's conclusion for far less noble reasons.

films.title: Primal Fear
films.note: The register Nayman reaches for: a self-contained, entertaining courtroom object, if a Bressonian one.

films.title: Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil
films.note: The other Southern-set Eastwood legal drama Juror No. 2 stands in relationship to.

films.title: The Taking of Pelham One Two Three
films.note: The staging rhyme for the final scene, except here it's unclear whether Collette, like Matthau, will call the cops.

segments.heading: The Clint Eastwood Mount Rushmore
segments.note: Four roles, eight contenders, spanning a half-century of American cinema

films.title: A Fistful of Dollars
films.note: The Man With No Name: an anchor face, contender No. 1.

films.title: For a Few Dollars More
films.note: Dollars trilogy, middle chapter of the No Name run.

films.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
films.note: The No Name who, the guys agree, grows up to be William Munny, which is why he has to be on the mountain.

films.title: Unforgiven
films.note: William Munny, locked in. 'I used to be a piece of shit. I still am.' The line is the thesis of the career and of Juror No. 2.

films.title: Dirty Harry
films.note: Locked in: the problematic fave with more to answer for than the Western characters, because now there are laws in the way.

films.title: Play Misty for Me
films.note: David Garver, one of the eight contenders.

films.title: High Plains Drifter
films.note: The Stranger, a contender.

films.title: The Outlaw Josey Wales
films.note: Josey Wales, a contender.

films.title: Every Which Way but Loose
films.note: Philo Beddoe, the reminder that Clint is humble enough to need an orangutan's help.

films.title: The Bridges of Madison County
films.note: Robert Kincaid, Sean's heart pick for the fourth slot, the one role that shows the romantic, artistic side.

films.title: Bronco Billy
films.note: Nayman's fourth-slot pitch: all he wants is to put on a show about America; Sean thinks it's bad.

films.title: Gran Torino
films.note: Walt Kowalski knocking on the door: empty-chair-era, halftime-in-America Clint, and a huge hit.

films.title: In the Line of Fire
films.note: The best film he's in that he never directed; Nayman would've taken an Oscar nod for it in '93.

films.title: Million Dollar Baby
films.note: Left off the mountain, but readable as a portrait of director-Clint, shepherding a team.

films.title: A Perfect World
films.note: The underrated one Sean pushes: Costner playing a Clint part, iconography built up and then upended.

films.title: Heretic
films.note: A24 thriller: two Mormon missionaries (Sophie Thatcher, Chloe East) knock on the door of Hugh Grant's Mr. Reed and get a philosophy lecture that turns into a trap. Grant in full freak mode, part Jordan Peterson, part professorial dickhead. Sean had a great time; some flaws once it tips toward hard genre.

films.title: The Boogeyman
films.note: Where you may remember Sophie Thatcher from.

films.title: The Fabelmans
films.note: Chloe East as young Sammy Fabelman's puckish girlfriend.

films.title: A Quiet Place
films.note: The pair's breakout script, engineered sonically for the theater, born from 'what's the worst thing if you couldn't make a sound.'

films.title: Haunt
films.note: The clever horror flick they directed before Heretic.

films.title: Contact
films.note: The origin story: at 12, both floored that a Zemeckis popcorn movie could hold an adult conversation about religion.

films.title: Inherit the Wind
films.note: A touchstone: Stanley Kramer's Spencer Tracy film, a discourse of ideas as drama.

films.title: Night of the Living Dead
films.note: Their model for horror as Trojan horse, here for race.

films.title: Invasion of the Body Snatchers
films.note: Same idea: genre smuggling in the Red Scare.

films.title: Death and the Maiden
films.note: A stage-derived Polanski film swirling in the contained-thriller inspiration pot.

films.title: Oldboy
films.note: Why they hired DP Chung-hoon Chung to keep the two-room movie from feeling small.

films.title: Last Night in Soho
films.note: More of Chung's work that sold them on him.

films.title: Wonka
films.note: Where Grant and Chung had just worked together, about as far from Heretic as a movie gets.

films.title: Cloud Atlas
films.note: The Grant performance that reminded them he could disappear into a role, once they pictured him as Mr. Reed.

films.title: Notting Hill
films.note: They read the dialogue in that Hugh Grant cadence and it got scarier.

films.title: Nightlight
films.note: The low-budget first feature that got the door open, step by step.

films.title: Here
films.note: Sean saw it the day before Heretic, Zemeckis's confined-space experiment, sharing Heretic's first four letters in the listings.

films.title: A Quiet Place: Day One
films.note: Michael Sarnoski's entry; he came to their theater's opening weekend.

films.title: Pig
films.note: The other Sarnoski film they name-check as a special-guest booking.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: Their friendly shot at hero Rian Johnson: selling a theatrical experience to Netflix sends eyeballs away from theaters like theirs.

films.title: Twisters
films.note: A blockbuster that worked for their Davenport theater.

films.title: Late Night with the Devil
films.note: The genre pickup that drew crowds for weeks, proof the love of genre still anchors.

films.title: The Remains of the Day
films.note: The James Ivory film Scott was floored by, and a lesson: it grossed $60M because people just showed up.

films.title: A.I. Artificial Intelligence
films.note: A thousand-times rewatch and huge favorite, and a movie feeling newly live as they think about AI in filmmaking.

films.title: Challengers
films.note: On their recent-favorites list.

films.title: Rebel Ridge
films.note: A lot of fun, per Brian.

films.title: Will & Harper
films.note: The documentary that destroyed them in the last five minutes.

films.title: Election
films.note: Produced by David Gale, the ex-MTV Films exec who first cracked the door for them.
